examples.text: Дул һеж биранда, а ил һел бара.

note.en: The 72-day cycle repeats 5 times a year. It starts on March 16 and ends on May 26. The period names remain the same on the subsequent cycles.

etymology.en: Masd. of *улбертара* "to have an eye on".
Lit. *to make of eye*. There's also *улбишарти* "cure from jinx".

etymology.en: Used with the verb *луккана* "to give" and likely stems from it.
Related to preverb *ьа-* "up". There are also *бети* "thither-ward", *цати* "hither-ward", but their usage is not entirely clear.
Recent semantic shift of the original meaning *that down there* (cf. *ьет* "that").
Could be related to *ьяҡьиҡьа* — Islamic sacrifice animal.

examples.text: Әвра аулши, һелдеҳна зарал ра калгун.

translation.en: He didn't work much, and therefore stayed healthy.
translation.ru: Он много не работал, потому и остался здоровым.

note.en: There are also *ьекь* "that up there", *ьех* "that down there".

translation.en: it doesn't rain but they grow anyway

etymology.en: From *ьеж* "this", cf *ьелттел* "like that" and *ьешттежил* "in this way".

note.en: There are also *ьекь* "this up here", *ьех* "this down here".

Related to *че* "on". Semantically corresponds to English suffix -ful.
